description: Swing GUI Builder v0.0.2 — Stabilize and enrich the VS Code extension: fix code generation bugs, respect project config, detect Java project structure, improve canvas UX, and prepare for Marketplace release.

userStories.title: Fix hexToRgb color validation in JavaGenerator
userStories.description: As a developer, I need hexToRgb to validate its input so that generated Java code never contains NaN in Color constructors.
acceptanceCriteria: hexToRgb() validates that input matches #RRGGBB format before converting
Invalid color strings throw an explicit error or return a documented fallback (e.g. Color.BLACK)
Generated .java files never contain `new Color(NaN, NaN, NaN)` for any input
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Enforce unique generated class names in JavaGenerator
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the code generator to ensure custom class names are unique so that generated Java code compiles without naming conflicts.
acceptanceCriteria: Generated class names for custom components are deterministically unique per canvas
Class names are decoupled from user-editable fields to prevent accidental collisions
A canvas with 10+ components of the same type produces compilable Java with unique class names
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Enforce unique variableName per component in canvas
userStories.description: As a developer, I need each component on the canvas to receive a unique variableName on creation so the generator never produces duplicate identifiers.
acceptanceCriteria: When a component is added to the canvas its variableName is auto-generated to be unique within that canvas
No two components on the same canvas share the same variableName
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Send project config defaults to webview on builder open
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the extension to read project and user settings and post them to the WebView when the builder opens so the canvas always knows the active configuration.
acceptanceCriteria: On builder open, CanvasPanel reads settings from .swingbuilder.json and VS Code settings
Config defaults (color, font, size) are posted to the WebView via a message before the first render
Precedence is respected: .swingbuilder.json > VS Code settings > internal defaults
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Apply config defaults when dropping components onto canvas
userStories.description: As a user, I want new components to be initialized with the configured defaults so I don't have to manually set color, font and size every time.
acceptanceCriteria: WebView stores the received config defaults from the extension
On component drop, new component properties (color, font, size) are initialized from the stored config defaults
Behavior is consistent between a new session and a reopened session
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add Java project structure detection service
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the extension to detect common Java project structures (Maven/Gradle, plain src/) so it can suggest a sensible default output folder.
acceptanceCriteria: Detection checks for src/main/java (Maven/Gradle standard) and a root-level src/ folder as Java source roots
Detected source root is exposed via ConfigReader or a dedicated utility function
For Maven/Gradle projects the suggested output folder is <java-root>/components
Function returns undefined/null when no known structure is found
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Infer package from output path and add folder picker fallback
userStories.description: As a user, I want the generated Java file to declare the correct package based on the output folder, and be prompted to choose a folder when the project structure is unrecognized.
acceptanceCriteria: package declaration is derived from the relative path of the output folder to the detected Java source root
For Maven/Gradle projects the package is 'components' (or matching sub-package) by default
When no project structure is detected a folder picker dialog is shown
.swingbuilder.json outputFolder setting suppresses the picker when set
Generated Java compiles with the correct package in both detected and manually-chosen scenarios
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Fix JSON schema contribution for .swingbuilder.json in package.json
userStories.description: As a user, I want autocomplete and inline validation when editing .swingbuilder.json in VS Code so I don't have to memorize all configuration options.
acceptanceCriteria: package.json jsonValidation entry correctly maps the .swingbuilder.json glob to the bundled schema file
Autocomplete and error markers appear when editing .swingbuilder.json with the installed extension
Schema validation works when loading the extension from a .vsix (not just in development mode)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Write README.md with full usage documentation
userStories.description: As a new user, I want a complete README so I can install, configure and use the extension without needing additional resources.
acceptanceCriteria: README.md contains: extension description, requirements (Java, VS Code version), installation steps, how-to-use guide, available commands list, and supported settings reference
A new Java developer can install the extension and generate valid Java code in under 5 minutes following only the README
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Complete CHANGELOG, LICENSE and package.json marketplace metadata
userStories.description: As a publisher, I need all marketplace metadata and release files to be complete so vsce package succeeds without warnings.
acceptanceCriteria: CHANGELOG.md is created with a 0.0.2 entry describing all changes
LICENSE has the correct year and holder with no placeholders
package.json has publisher, categories, icon, repository, homepage and keywords filled in coherently
vsce package generates a .vsix without errors or critical warnings
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Add command toolbar to builder UI
userStories.description: As a user, I want a toolbar inside the builder with buttons for New, Open, Save, Generate and Init Config so I can access all main actions without the Command Palette.
acceptanceCriteria: Toolbar is visible in the builder with buttons: New, Open, Save, Generate, Init Config
Each button posts a message to the extension that triggers the same handler as the corresponding VS Code command
Buttons render in a disabled state when the action is unavailable in the current context
Generate button shows a spinner or temporary feedback text while generation is in progress
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Implement component deletion via Delete/Backspace and properties panel
userStories.description: As a user, I want to remove a selected component by pressing Delete or Backspace (or via a button) so I can quickly clean up my canvas.
acceptanceCriteria: Pressing Delete or Backspace while a component is selected removes it from the canvas
A delete button in the properties panel also removes the currently selected component
Deleting a component updates the persisted canvas state
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add variable name validation with visual feedback in properties panel
userStories.description: As a user, I want to be warned immediately when I enter an invalid or duplicate variable name so I can fix it before generating code.
acceptanceCriteria: variableName field validates: no spaces, starts with a letter or underscore, no Java reserved words
A duplicate variableName across canvas components triggers a red border or inline error on the field
The Generate action is blocked (button disabled or error notification shown) while any name is invalid or duplicated
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Implement canvas zoom and pan
userStories.description: As a user, I want to zoom in/out and pan the canvas viewport so I can work comfortably with large layouts.
acceptanceCriteria: Ctrl+scroll zooms the canvas in and out
Zoom-in and zoom-out buttons in the toolbar or canvas UI also control zoom level
Middle-click drag or a dedicated pan mode allows moving the viewport
Component selection and drag-and-drop work correctly at any zoom level
Component x, y, width, height values are stored as absolute coordinates independent of zoom
Generated Java uses absolute coordinates unaffected by the current zoom level
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Add resize handles to canvas components
userStories.description: As a user, I want to drag edge/corner handles on selected components to resize them visually so I don't have to type width and height manually.
acceptanceCriteria: Selecting a component shows resize handles on its borders and corners
Dragging a handle updates width and height in real time on the canvas
The properties panel reflects the new dimensions immediately during drag
Resized dimensions are persisted correctly when saving the layout
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Implement undo/redo history for canvas actions
userStories.description: As a user, I want to undo and redo canvas actions (add, delete, move, resize, property change) so I can recover from mistakes.
acceptanceCriteria: Ctrl+Z undoes the last action (add, delete, move, resize, property change)
Ctrl+Shift+Z and Ctrl+Y redo the last undone action
History covers at least the last 50 actions to avoid unbounded memory growth
History is not required to persist between sessions
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Improve error handling — replace silent catch blocks in extension.ts
userStories.description: As a developer, I want all errors to be logged or surfaced with actionable messages so no failure is silently swallowed.
acceptanceCriteria: All silent catch blocks in extension.ts are replaced with error logging to the Output Channel or a VS Code notification
Critical generation errors appear as VS Code notifications with a descriptive message indicating what failed
Non-critical errors are written to the extension Output Channel without showing popups
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Set up test infrastructure and write initial unit tests
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a runnable test suite covering the critical bug fixes introduced in v0.0.2 so regressions are caught automatically.
acceptanceCriteria: Test suite runs via `npm test` or equivalent command
Tests cover hexToRgb: valid #RRGGBB input, invalid input, and boundary cases
Tests cover unique name generation: multiple same-type components produce unique names
Tests cover config default precedence: .swingbuilder.json > VS Code settings > internal defaults
All tests pass consistently
Typecheck passes
Tests pass
